architecture_fit.note: Single attention layer, no MLP, one fixed cross-group additive mask — a minimal delta from base_model's causal mask that yields clean bipartite attention (within=0, between=0.125).

baseline_comparison.note: Two decisive strawmen: no-mask baseline (score -0.0006, retrieval 0.21) and a learned content-only projection that overfits, both failing where the mask succeeds.

faithfulness.note: Mask on/off ablation directly toggles the behaviour, and the held-out-seed generalisation test (mask 0.52 vs content-only 0.0) gives strong causal evidence the circuit is positional.

operating_range.note: Robust (1.0) across the full num_heads sweep and to a held-out seed, but flat by construction (0.125=1/group_size) and never probes group_size/seq_len scale.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-set circuit — the bipartite mask is written out by hand and reproduces the target behaviour, demonstrating clear mechanistic understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Three complementary views (cross-group heatmaps, mechanism-vs-baseline score bars, overfit-vs-transfer generalisation curve), each killing one alternative explanation.

visualisation_rationale.note: README explicitly ties each chart to the goal's claim — heatmap blocks = bipartite definition, bars = baseline contrast, curve = positional necessity.

notes: A clean, honest hand-built circuit: one fixed cross-group mask (minimal delta from base_model) produces perfect bipartite separation, with a mask-ablation and a learned-content-only overfitting strawman providing decisive baseline and faithfulness evidence. The attempt also correctly diagnoses that the shipped task.evaluate is broken (np.softmax, ignores model_fn) and faithfully re-implements the documented payload contract. Main weakness is operating range — robustness is shown only across num_heads (flat by construction) and one held-out seed, not across input-scale axes like group_size.
